betterquesting:10.desc:8:
My intentions were good. I believed I was helping, and yet my fate has led me taking me to this Mission that I rejected and fought against. 
To the eyes of my past self, I may be the villain. And yet, it is this journey that led me becoming who I am now. 
And this journey was consacrated. By Love. By the Realmshifters who, despite intuiting where my steps would take me, decided to maintain their gift of Alchemy.
A gift they coudl have so easily taken back, as they did to Clunkhead, as they did to Kror. 
Because they recognized that, no matter the destination, the journey, MY journey, was one worth traversing. It was a good thing that I would become who I am now.
This is humbling, to a degree words cannot express, but I will try... 
Someone - no not someone - a Fractallite, one capable of touching reality to its most basic level, saw an overconfident scientist, paying the price of their ambition, and decided that their journey, one that would take them to the extremes of the Universe, not only was a worthwile journey, but one that would be worth consacrating.
I was chosen. Not by the Meatball Man's partial reading of the Arbiter's prophecies, not by the Elder Ones giving me recursion, not by the Faithful Undead who knew my destiny. 
I was chosen by someone who then let me go my own way. Trusting onto me one of the greatest powers, which in no small part contributed to my Ascension.
No, not someone. Not one person...
